# Agentic User Mandate Protocol

> AUMP v0.1 is a working-draft mandate layer for user authority, preference,
> disclosure, negotiation, escalation, and evidence in AI agents acting on
> behalf of a principal.

## Canonical repositories

- Specification: https://github.com/Agentic-User-Mandate-Protocol/spec
- Conformance: https://github.com/Agentic-User-Mandate-Protocol/conformance
- Python SDK: https://github.com/Agentic-User-Mandate-Protocol/aump-py
- TypeScript SDK: https://github.com/Agentic-User-Mandate-Protocol/aump-js
- Examples: https://github.com/Agentic-User-Mandate-Protocol/examples
- Site: https://github.com/Agentic-User-Mandate-Protocol/site

## Protocol position

AUMP does not replace MCP, A2A, UCP, or AP2. MCP exposes tools and context.
A2A lets agents discover and communicate with other agents. UCP models commerce
capabilities and checkout surfaces. AP2 carries payment mandates. AUMP defines
the mandate an agent must obey before it calls a tool, sends an agent message,
negotiates terms, discloses private information, commits a deal, escalates to a
trusted user interface, or records evidence.

## First integration rule

Put `evaluate_action` before every material action. If the decision is
`allowed`, the runtime may proceed. If it is `requires_escalation`, the runtime
must pause and ask through a trusted channel. If it is `denied`, the runtime
must not take the action.

## Package entry points

- Python CLI: `uv tool install aump`, then `aump validate`,
  `aump validate-evidence`, `aump validate-bridge`, or
  `aump evaluate-action`.
- Conformance CLI: `uv tool install aump-conformance`, then
  `aump-conformance validate`.
- Examples CLI: `uv tool install aump-examples`, then
  `aump-examples marketplace`.
- TypeScript CLI: `npx @agentic-user-mandate-protocol/aump --help`.

## Runtime proof

The marketplace example proves the control boundary without an LLM API key. It
builds an A2A message with an AUMP mandate reference, validates the inbound
mandate hash, rejects full private-mandate leaks, evaluates MCP-shaped action
calls, enforces budget and disclosure denial, escalates checkout-ready
transitions, and appends canonical evidence events.
